pattern: Each operation shifts two elements by plus and minus two, so parity is preserved; matching sorted odds to odds and evens to evens minimizes the total upward movement.
hints: Adding two to one element and subtracting two from another never changes any element's parity, so odds stay odd and evens stay even.
Split both arrays by parity and sort each group; the cheapest matching pairs the smallest with the smallest.
Sum the positive gaps (where target exceeds nums) across both parity groups; since each gap moves in steps of two and each operation supplies one increase and one decrease, divide that total by four.

edge_inputs.description: mixed parities must be matched within their parity class
values already a permutation need zero ops
large gap within one parity class

stress.description: random arrays where target is a parity-preserving rearrangement of an adjusted nums
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 500000) for _ in range(n)]
    target = []
    for v in nums:
        delta = 2 * rng.randint(-50, 50)
        nv = v + delta
        if nv < 1:
            nv = v
        target.append(nv)
    rng.shuffle(target)
    return [nums, target]
stress.note: target preserves each element's parity so a valid matching exists
